forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system south of Western Australia will move east slowly over the weekend to be near Tasmania early next week. A trough is expected to develop across northern SA early next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area and near far western coasts. Maximum temperatures near to below average. South to southeasterly winds.
Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area and near western coasts. Southwest to southeasterly winds.
Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area and near far western coasts. Maximum temperatures near average. South to southeasterly winds.
Isolated light showers about the southern and western coasts. Isolated showers developing about the far north. Maximum temperatures near average. East to southeast winds.

text.p: Patchy morning fog in the west. Isolated showers and possible thunderstorms across the far north, extending south into central districts during the day. Maximum temperatures near average. East to southeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: less than 2 mm across the northern agricultural area and the west coast, increasing to 2 to 5 mm across the southern agricultural area and the North East Pastoral district. Higher totals of 5 to 10 mm are possible across windward coasts and ranges and with thunderstorms in the northeast.
